allies.rationale_text: Metacognitive-questioning helps circle-of-competence inspect whether current judgment is genuinely earned or only feels familiar.
allies.source_quote: It relies heavily on metacognition (thinking about thinking), enabling an expert to frame a problem and reflect on whether their proposed answer seems reasonable.

allies.rationale_text: The Feynman technique helps circle-of-competence expose the perimeter by revealing what cannot yet be explained simply.
allies.source_quote: The Feynman Technique: This powerful learning framework is a direct tool for defining the perimeter of the CoC.

antagonists.rationale_text: Authority-bias can pull judgment outside the earned perimeter by making borrowed expertise feel sufficient.
antagonists.source_quote: circle-of-competence vs authority-bias: circle-of-competence conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when external expert status is accepted outside proven domain boundaries.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias makes circle-of-competence count only the evidence that flatters the current boundary story.
antagonists.source_quote: When relying on the CoC, a person may seek information that only confirms their pre-existing framing of the problem.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Circle-of-competence conflicts with authority-bias when external expert status is accepted outside proven domain boundaries.
structured_tensions.source_quote: circle-of-competence vs authority-bias: circle-of-competence conflicts with authority-bias (authority bias) when external expert status is accepted outside proven domain boundaries.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Circle-of-competence conflicts with commitment-bias when teams keep operating outside capability limits to protect prior decisions.
structured_tensions.source_quote: circle-of-competence vs commitment-bias: circle-of-competence conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when teams keep operating outside capability limits to protect prior decisions.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Circle-of-competence conflicts with confirmation-bias when practitioners selectively count successes to confirm they are operating inside their competence circle rather than honestly mapping its edges.
structured_tensions.source_quote: circle-of-competence vs confirmation-bias: circle-of-competence conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when practitioners selectively count successes to confirm they are operating inside their competence circle rather than honestly mapping its edges.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source gives unusually explicit first-order conflict language, so the file preserves those tensions directly and keeps allies close to the named perimeter-check tools.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation sprawl beyond the named self-monitoring and bias-conflict surface.
open_questions: Dunning-Kruger and curse-of-knowledge remain source-real antagonists, but this pass kept the stronger direct conflicts and named perimeter allies.
